channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Unit Test AI Guide — Zero Hallucination, Cross-Stack Standard
item.description: Focus: Unit Tests ONLY — no integration, no E2E Stacks: Node.js (NestJS/Express) · React.js · Python · Angular · Laravel Goal: AI generates unit tests consistently, deterministically, without...

item.title: How to Reduce Your JavaScript Bundle Size by 70% in 10 Steps
item.description: Most JavaScript applications ship far more code than necessary. The average web page loads 1.7 MB of JavaScript (HTTP Archive, 2025), but developers only use about 35% of the code they deploy....

item.title: Why Security Engineers Need to Think Beyond Code
item.description: The CIA Triad Isn't Just Jargon. It's Your Daily Reality. Most developers learn security as an afterthought. You write code first, then maybe add authentication, throw in some encryption if you're...

item.title: Listening to the Heartbeat of Your Database: Understanding Change Data Capture (CDC)
item.description: CDC stands for Change Data Capture. What You Will Learn What CDC is The story behind it How it works internally Common use cases A simplified implementation example What Is CDC? Change Data...

item.title: Your LLM reads the whole file. It doesn't have to.
item.description: Coding agents read specs, design docs, and long READMEs every day. Most of the time, they only need a few sections. Yet they load the entire file into context. The hidden cost of "just read the...

item.title: pnpm workspaces in a monorepo: the setup that survived CI on Railway and the problems the docs don't warn you about
item.description: pnpm workspaces in a monorepo: the setup that survived CI on Railway and the problems the docs don't warn you about The correct fix for speeding up installs in a TypeScript monorepo is adding more...

item.title: pnpm workspaces en monorepo: el setup que sobrevivió CI en Railway y los problemas que los docs no anticipan
item.description: pnpm workspaces en monorepo: el setup que sobrevivió CI en Railway y los problemas que los docs no anticipan La solución correcta para acelerar installs en un monorepo TypeScript es agregar más...

item.title: Telegram Channels Need Project Pins
item.description: Telegram Channels Need Project Pins APC is the portable context layer. APX is the daily runtime that makes that context usable. Telegram fits that split well: APC defines what a project is, and...

item.title: I Built an Open-Source API Gateway for 100+ AI Models at $0.14/M Tokens
item.description: TL;DR AgentHub is a unified, OpenAI-compatible API gateway for 100+ models (DeepSeek, Qwen, Claude, etc.) at $0.14/M tokens — about 99% cheaper than GPT-4. Register (1M free tokens):...

item.title: Embedding AI Workflows: Building Our Orquesta SDK
item.description: Originally published at orquesta.live/blog/embedding-ai-workflows-orquesta-sdk Embedding AI-powered workflows into existing web applications should be as seamless as possible. This philosophy...

item.title: In season 2 of Sugar, Colin Farrell’s quirky detective becomes much more human
item.description: When Colin Farrell was doing press for the first season of the detective series Sugar, he had to be very careful with how he spoke. Sugar is a story about a quirky private detective, but it's also...

item.title: Kaleidescape’s movie player blows streaming, and your wallet, away
item.description: We've lost something in the past 15 years. Netflix, Amazon, Disney, Apple; they've all convinced us that streaming is the best way to watch movies and shows at home. With everything at our...

item.title: Anthropic’s Fable and the State of AI
item.description: On June 9th, Anthropic released its Fable generative AI model. Three days later, the US government classified it as a dangerous munition, and used its export-control authority to prohibit any...

item.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
item.description: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language...

item.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
item.description: The first wave of enterprise AI concern was straightforward. It was simply employees pasting sensitive data into public AI tools. Security teams responded with usage policies, domain blocks, and...

item.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
item.description: Salesforce has revealed that it disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration within its platform in response to a security incident impacting the competitive intelligence company on June 11,...

item.title: The inevitable weakness of metrics
item.description: There are plenty of useful things a metric can reveal. There are even more it can obscure or corrupt. It took me well over a decade of tracking my own life in ever greater detail to fully...

item.title: Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
item.description: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable...

item.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China. ASML says it isn’t
item.description: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

item.title: Apple Patches Beats Studio Buds Flaw Letting Nearby Attackers Spy via Microphone
item.description: Apple has updated its Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds to patch a high-severity vulnerability that could be exploited by nearby hackers to eavesdrop on users. The vulnerability, tracked as...

item.title: Barret Zoph is out at OpenAI again after just five months
item.description: Five months after returning to OpenAI, Barret Zoph - the company's head of enterprise AI sales - has departed, The Verge has learned. Zoph returned to OpenAI in mid-January after a stint as...

item.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
item.description: DeductiveAI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.

item.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
item.description: Crypto Clipper spreads over USB and communicates over Tor.

item.title: How the Peter Thiel-Linked Dialog Club Secretly Ranks Its Members
item.description: Leaked files show the invite-only network grades members by their money and fame, shaping who’s in, who’s out, and who pays.

item.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
item.description: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.

item.title: The White House Is Making Up Its Rules for AI in Real Time
item.description: Anthropic still can’t distribute Claude Mythos or Fable 5 after running afoul of the Trump administration. But no one can say exactly what the company did wrong.

item.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
item.description: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.

item.title: Valve is so behind on Steam Controller orders that some won’t ship until 2027
item.description: Valve has some good news and bad news about Steam Controllers. The good news: If you make a reservation for a Steam Controller, the company will now show you one of three estimates of when you'll...

item.title: You can now use the Game Boy Camera with your phone
item.description: The $50 GB Operator is an accessory that lets you connect, play, and authenticate Game Boy, Game Boy Color, and Game Boy Advance cartridges on PCs and other devices. Now it's getting some new...

item.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
item.description: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.
